_doc: Retail-side field spec for the execution+dataflow trace (docs/plans/trace-tooling-phase-b.md, the B2 LOGIC drill-in). Maps each instrumented engine VA -> the declared payload the Frida agent reads at that call, joined to the port's CALL_TRACE_* fields by (va, field-name). Consumed by tools/flow_diff.py.

_schema.key: engine VA (0x-hex or decimal). Matches the port's CALL_TRACE_BEGIN(va).
_schema.name: short label for flow_diff output (wins over functions.csv).
_schema.chain_benign: true => exclude this VA's POSITION from chain alignment (a clock read / harness-substituted poll that legitimately floats). No field payload needed.
_schema.fields[].src: global (read DAT at `va`) | arg (Frida onEnter arg index) | argderef (deref arg[index] + `off`) | chain (global root + pointer hops) | rngcalls (cumulative LCG-draw count = the agent's g_rng_count_total; auto-installs the single FUN_005bf505 hook — the RNG-CONSUMPTION signal unified into the flow trace, no ad-hoc flag) | renderid (the blit SOURCE cel's cross-side identity from the render_id registry; reads ECX (__thiscall this) or args[index] for a cdecl blit, then `key`:res|frame; auto-installs the FUN_00418470 resolver hook — the DDraw blit-trace identity, Phase-B B3, mirrors src/render_id.c) | thisderef (read a field off ECX + `off`: the __thiscall `this` object's own field at the call's onEnter) | thischain (like chain but ROOTED at ECX, not a global: start at ECX, follow each `hops` pointer hop, read typed at `off` — reaches a field behind a this-pointer, e.g. an actor's render-state at *(actor+0x40)+off) | retval (onLeave).
_schema.fields[].key: for src=renderid: which identity component to return — res (resource_id) | frame.
_schema.fields[].va: for src=global/chain: the DAT_ VA to read (chain reads it as the root pointer).
_schema.fields[].index: for src=arg/argderef: Frida InvocationArguments index, 0-BASED (index 0 = first STACK param).
_schema.fields[].off: for src=argderef/chain: byte offset added before the final typed read.
_schema.fields[].hops: for src=chain: list of byte offsets, each a pointer hop after reading the global root pointer at `va`; the final `off` is the value read. chain = read *(va), then for each h: p = *(p + h), then read typed at p + off. Reaches a field behind a global root (e.g. the camera/view at *(*(DAT_008a9b50) + 0x104c) + off).
_schema.fields[].benign: true => compare for PRESENCE only (memory-layout pointer, phase-origin counter, RNG seed origin); pair with `reason`.
_schema.fields[].reason: human note surfaced by --field-timeline when a benign field diverges (accepted-with-cause, not silently dropped).

_caveat_arg_index: SotES is MSVC; many engine functions are __thiscall/__fastcall (this/first arg in ECX, not on the stack). For those, Frida's args[0] is the first STACK param, NOT `this` — validate any src=arg/argderef field against a live --textout-probe/mem_watch read before a port depends on it. src=global reads (a DAT_ VA) have no such ambiguity and are the safe default for state-machine stubs; prefer them. Validated convention will be pinned here per-VA as fields land.

0x5b8fc0.name: flip
0x5b8fc0._note: FUN_005b8fc0 — the DDraw Flip dispatcher, entered ONCE PER FRAME (it is the agent's flip-frame anchor). The right cross-side per-frame VA: retail's title runner FUN_0056aea0 keeps its do/while loop INTERNAL, so onEnter there fires once at scene entry, not per frame; the port externalized that loop, so the shared per-frame boundary both sides hit is the Flip. Port side: src/main.c drive_present (CALL_TRACE_BEGIN(0x5b8fc0)).

fields.name: rng
fields._note: DAT_008a4f94 — the engine LCG state word (srand FUN_005bf4fb seeds it; seeded to OSS_RNG_DEFAULT_SEED on both sides under --seed-pin). Read at the Flip = the frame-boundary RNG snapshot. A [data] divergence here the frame after a mismatched draw localizes a stray RNG consumption (parity pillar 3, docs/parity-model.md).

fields.name: rngcalls
fields.reason: cumulative LCG (FUN_005bf505) draw count at the Flip = per-frame RNG-consumption total. Benign for now (RNG order parity is deferred until every in-scene RNG consumer is RE'd, per the ckpt-73 decision); surfaced so flow_diff can localize WHERE consumption diverges once we match it 1:1.

fields.name: tint
fields.reason: DAT_008a93fc — the global palette-tint / time-of-day index FUN_00417c40 switches on (in_ECX+0x289c == &DAT_008a760c-0xaac+0x289c == 0x8a93fc). 0 = case-0 IDENTITY (no recolor); 1 = warm, 2/3 = darken, 4/5 = the FUN_004182d0 complex ramp. The port's tile/parallax resolve uses the plain getter 0x418470 (no tint); whether the in-game town needs the tint hinges on this value (PORT-DEBT render-palette-tint).

fields.name: lutgate1
fields.reason: in_ECX+0x29b0 — FUN_00417c40's secondary-LUT gate A. When (0x8a9510 != 0 || 0x8a9514 != 1000) the getter remaps every palette index through the 256-byte LUT at 0x8a9410 before returning the cel (the 'darker/more-saturated' color post the port's 0x418470 skips).

fields.name: lutgate2
fields.reason: in_ECX+0x29b4 — FUN_00417c40's secondary-LUT gate B (the 1000 = identity baseline; != 1000 arms the LUT).

fields.name: lut000
fields.reason: LUT bytes [0..3] (in_ECX+0x28b0). Identity LUT => 0x03020100. A non-identity value confirms an index remap is loaded.

fields.name: lut064
fields.reason: LUT bytes [0x40..0x43]; identity => 0x43424140.

fields.name: lut128
fields.reason: LUT bytes [0x80..0x83]; identity => 0x83828180.

fields.name: lut192
fields.reason: LUT bytes [0xc0..0xc3]; identity => 0xc3c2c1c0.

fields.name: cam_mapw
fields.reason: exploratory camera/view probe (in-game-intro.md): view[0x00] = map pixel width = dim0*0xc80, set at 586010:856. No port equivalent yet — retail ground-truth for the camera build.

fields.name: cam_maph
fields.reason: view[0x04] = map pixel height = dim1*0xc80, 586010:862.

fields.name: cam_x34
fields.reason: view+0x34 — scroll-x sub-pixel accumulator (projector sx term); zeroed at room init via 587d30, driven by the camera follow. The value at the first town frame tells us if the camera snapped to the spawn.

fields.name: cam_x4c
fields.reason: view+0x4c — scroll-y sub-pixel accumulator (projector sy term); zeroed at room init via 587d30, driven by the follow.

fields.name: cam_x5c
fields.reason: view+0x5c — y scroll origin (projector sy term); set 0 at 586010:858.

fields.name: cam_x60
fields.reason: view+0x60 — x scroll origin (projector sx + window col0); set 0 at 586010:857.

fields.name: cam_x64
fields.reason: view+0x64 — viewport width (window ncols, /0xc80); set 64000 (=640*100) at 586010:860.

fields.name: cam_x68
fields.reason: view+0x68 — viewport height (window nrows, /0xc80); set 48000 (=480*100) at 586010:861.

fields.name: cam_x74
fields.reason: view+0x74 — zoom/vertical-shear term (projector sy: +cam+0x74*100); set 0 at 586010:859, updated from a controller's +0x4c at 499ab0:96.

fields.name: cam_x1c
fields.reason: view+0x1c (view[7]) — camera pan state flag; 439690 zeroes it on a snap/pan command. Probing to model the pan.

fields.name: cam_x20
fields.reason: view+0x20 (view[8]) — the camera follow VELOCITY CAP (= pan speed); the easer FUN_0043d1d0 ramps vel +10/frame up to this. The town pan = 300 (set once with the target at hold-end). Ported: camera_view.cap.

fields.name: cam_x6c
fields.reason: view+0x6c (view[0x1b]) — TARGET x scroll origin (clamped to [0, mapw-vpw]); RESOLVED: a SCRIPTED constant (12800 for the town), set once at hold-end, NOT leader-tracking. The easer FUN_0043d1d0 moves +0x60 toward it. Ported: camera_view.tgt_x.

fields.name: cam_x70
fields.reason: view+0x70 (view[0x1c]) — TARGET y scroll origin (clamped to [0, maph-vph]); pairs with cam_x6c (12800, static through the town pan). Ported: camera_view.tgt_y.

0x43d1d0.name: camera_follow_step
0x43d1d0._note: FUN_0043d1d0 (366 B) — the once-per-frame camera EASE-TO-TARGET, called from 0x439690:1123 (then FUN_00499ab0 shake/HUD). Dispatched through a HEAP function pointer (invisible to static call-graph search); located ckpt 69 with a HW watchpoint on view+0x60 (writer insn 0x43d26d). Per axis: dist=|tgt-cur|; if vel<dist -> cur += sign*(vel + (flag&&dist>16000 ? (dist-16000)/10 : 0)), vel=min(vel+10,cap); else cur=tgt (snap), vel=max(vel-10,0). Then FUN_0043d340 writes the projector accumulators +0x34/+0x4c (shake; inert during the pan). PORT MIRROR: src/camera_follow.c camera_follow_step (host-tested bit-exact vs the captured retail trajectory); add CALL_TRACE_BEGIN(0x43d1d0) when it is wired into the live game_drive camera (PORT-DEBT ingame-camera-pan). Fields read at onEnter (the view = *(*(0x8a9b50)+0x104c)): the X-axis easer state.

fields.name: cur_x
fields.reason: view+0x60 — current x scroll (the easer's output; matches cam_x60 at the Flip). camera_view.cur_x.

fields.name: tgt_x
fields.reason: view+0x6c — target x scroll. camera_view.tgt_x.

fields.name: vel_x
fields.reason: view+0x08 — the X follow VELOCITY accumulator (+10/frame, cap=+0x20); previously unprobed, now the integrator. camera_view.vel_x.

fields.name: vel_y
fields.reason: view+0x0c — the Y follow velocity accumulator. camera_view.vel_y.

fields.name: cap
fields.reason: view+0x20 — velocity cap / pan speed (300 for the town). camera_view.cap (== cam_x20).

fields.name: flag
fields.reason: view+0x1c — far-boost enable (0 for the town pan -> no (dist-16000)/10 term). camera_view.flag (== cam_x1c).

0x5bf505.name: rand_draw
0x5bf505._note: FUN_005bf505 (30 B) — the engine LCG step (MSVC rand: DAT_008a4f94 = DAT*0x343fd + 0x269ec3; return DAT>>0x10 & 0x7fff). Hooked as a FLOW-TRACE entry so each draw's auto-captured `ret_va` NAMES THE CONSUMER SITE — the scene-wide RNG-consumer census (Phase 2, the USER directive that retires the ckpt-73 defer-all-RNG). Capture over a TIGHT --call-trace-frames whitelist (HOT: the scene-load tick draws a burst, then a few per hold tick), then histogram `ret_va` (group by seq/sim_tick for the consumption ORDER) to enumerate every in-scene RNG consumer; attribute each ret_va to its function via the decompile, then annotate THAT producer with a `rngcalls` field + a port-side CALL_TRACE_BEGIN mirror and match consumption order both sides (rng+rngcalls per anchor). Known town consumers to expect: the EFFECT-band update (0xe29a wander) + the spawn (townsfolk facing/idle-phase, FUN_0041f200), the fountain/ambient particles (band +0x13e0 / 0x493480), and 0x54f980 (CHARACTER behaviour, scene-locked at the hold). engine-quirk #77; docs/parity-model.md pillar 3. NB this is the discovery half; the rngcalls field on annotated producers (0x46cd70/0x54f980 etc.) is the matching half. Supersedes the ad-hoc --rand-probe for the in-scene census.

fields.name: rng_state
fields.reason: DAT_008a4f94 read onEnter = the LCG state BEFORE this draw (next = state*0x343fd+0x269ec3; returned value = next>>0x10 & 0x7fff). With `seq` it pins the consumption ORDER, so a cross-side divergence localizes to the FIRST mismatched draw.

0x46cd70.name: actor_update_all
0x46cd70._note: FUN_0046cd70 (1031 B) — the per-SIM-TICK actor-update master driver, called once per tick from the in-game loop 0x439690 as FUN_0046cd70(1) (when *(param+0x1c)==0). Walks every actor-pool band off DAT_008a9b50 (active = actor+0x1d0 != 0): 0x1160 (0x20), 0x1060 (0x40), 0x13e0 (0x400), 0x23e0 (0x60), and the MAIN band 0x11e0 (0x80=128 slots). For each main actor it ticks timers (+0x2c4, +0x2ec), then calls FUN_0054f980(actor+0x40, actor+0x40, 0, 0) for the primary render-state entry and FUN_0054f980(entry-0x294, entry, 1, idx) for each sub-entry (the kinematic body-part chain) — that is where the animation advances. This is the once-per-tick clock the actor anim rides; it is NOT the render/emit pass (FUN_0048c150). Determinism: the anim counter is a pure function of (sim-ticks since clip-set), so it pins on the SAME g_sim_tick anchor as the camera (game_enter scene-load reset) — engine-quirk #76. No port mirror yet (actors unported, PORT-DEBT present-actor-modes).

fields.name: frame_ctr
fields.reason: the god-object base pointer; presence-only. The per-tick frame counter +0x276c is bumped here (& 0x7fff) once per tick.

fields.name: rng
fields.reason: DAT_008a4f94 — the shared LCG state word, snapshotted at the per-SIM-TICK actor-update boundary (0x46cd70 onEnter). Determinism probe (ckpt 73): under --seed-pin the seed is identical both runs, so if the shared stream is consumed in the same ORDER per sim-tick, rng at tick N matches run-to-run. It does NOT (0/8643 ticks, quirk #77): a non-deterministic-rate consumer (a per-PRESENT draw x the non-deterministic presents-per-tick, quirk #75) perturbs the stream phase the per-tick actor draws then see — the ~6.7k-px actor-band residual (0x54f980 draws this LCG for idle waits + wander offsets). parity pillar 3, docs/parity-model.md.

fields.name: rngcalls
fields.reason: cumulative LCG draw count at the per-sim-tick actor-update boundary — the RNG-consumption signal unified into the flow trace (openrecet rngcalls pattern). Pairs with `rng` (state): once every in-scene RNG consumer is RE'd, matching rng+rngcalls per anchor on both sides is how the actor band reaches 1:1 (deferred per ckpt 73).

0x54f980.name: actor_update
0x54f980._note: FUN_0054f980 (11597 B) — the per-actor update / behaviour dispatch, called per render-state entry from FUN_0046cd70 (so once per SIM-TICK). It (1) shadow-copies the render-state param_1->param_2 (the kinematic chain: a sub-entry inherits its parent's transform), then (2) switches on the actor TYPE in_ECX[0x75] (= actor+0x1d4, the 0x1131e.. behaviour id). EVERY animating case runs the SAME inline frame-stepper on the render-state's anim fields (+0x6c clip / +0x70 timer / +0x72 frame / +0x74 done): timer++; if timer>=clip[+0x44](dur){ frame++; timer=0; if frame>=clip[+0x42](count){ clip[+0x48]==0 ? frame=clip[+0x152](loop_to) : (hold last: frame=count-1, done=1, timer=1) } }. The clip (+0x6c) is set on STATE CHANGE by FUN_0040afe0/FUN_0041e600 (reset timer/frame/done, but only when the clip actually changes). PORT MIRROR: src/anim_clip.c anim_clip_advance + anim_state_set (host-tested); the clip descriptor layout (count@0x42, dur@0x44, oneshot@0x48, loop_to@0x152, per-frame sprite delta + x/y offsets) is anim_clip. Fields below read actor SLOT 0's primary render-state anim block (representative). engine-quirk #76; findings/in-game-intro.md 'The actor animation cycle'.

fields.name: a0_clip
fields.reason: actor[0] render-state +0x6c — current animation clip ptr (anim_state.clip). 0 = inert.

fields.name: a0_timer
fields.reason: actor[0] render-state +0x70 — frame timer (low u16; sim-ticks in the current frame). anim_state.timer.

fields.name: a0_frame
fields.reason: actor[0] render-state +0x72 — current frame index (low u16). anim_state.frame; advances once per sim-tick, deterministic under the g_sim_tick anchor.

fields.name: code
fields.reason: in_ECX+0x1d4 = the actor behaviour code (the 0x54f980 switch selector); filter for ambient codes 0x11365-0x1137x.

fields.name: p3
fields.reason: param_3 — the sub-cel flag (0 = primary update; the sound cases break when !=0).

fields.name: cd5c
fields.reason: *(param_2+0x5c) = puVar4+0x5c, the inline-sound-case countdown (case 0x11367/0x11379). Meaningful only for ambient codes.

0x491ae0.name: actor_render_emit
0x491ae0._note: FUN_00491ae0 (2599 B) — the per-actor RENDER/EMIT pass for the MAIN actor band (DAT_008a9b50+0x11e0, 0x80 slots = the town NPCs + party), called FUN_00491ae0(sub_idx=0, view) per active slot (actor+0x1d0!=0) from the per-frame render driver FUN_0048c150:52 (the in_ECX[7]==0 free-roam branch). __thiscall: ECX = the ACTOR object; args[0]=sub-entry index (0 for the primary), args[1]=the view (param_2). Body = a switch on the actor TYPE/behaviour code ECX+0x1d4 (uVar11): cases 0x11256/0x11260/0x112ec/0x112f6/0x11314/0x1131e/0x1133c/0x11346/0x11350/0x1872d/0x1872f + default 0x11257. The 0x1872d arm is the ANIMATED-CHARACTER case (reads the render-state clip ptr +0x6c / frame +0x72 — quirk #76's 3rd witness, base+delta sprite from the 0x154-B clip). The common tail LAB_004923eb builds draw nodes from a 10-dword stack descriptor and emits via FUN_00417c40(bank,frame)+FUN_00492670 (the actor analog of map_render_walk's draw_pool_emit). NO PORT MIRROR yet (PORT-DEBT present-actor-modes / present-actor-modes); these fields are RETAIL GROUND TRUTH to pin which arm the town NPCs hit + their render-state, so the port emits present modes 0/1/2. Fields: ECX (actor) own fields via thisderef; the primary render-state (*(ECX+0x40)+0*0x294) via thischain hops=[0x40].

fields.name: b1d4
fields.reason: actor+0x1d4 — the behaviour/TYPE code (the 0x491ae0 switch key uVar11; also FUN_0054f980's dispatch key in_ECX[0x75]). THE datum: which render arm + which 0x54f980 behaviour the town NPC runs. 0x1872d = animated character.

fields.name: active1d0
fields.reason: actor+0x1d0 — the active gate (0x48c150/0x46cd70 only walk a slot when this != 0). Confirms the slot is live.

fields.name: count44
fields.reason: actor+0x44 — u16 count of render-state entries (the kinematic body-part chain; 0x46cd70/0x48c150 loop sub-entries 1..count-1 at +0x294 stride).

fields.name: layer_fc
fields.reason: actor+0xfc — u16 draw-pool LAYER index (the 0x1872f arm emits into *(view+0x54)+layer*8 = draw_pool layer; the actor's draw-order key).

fields.name: ctx30
fields.reason: actor+0x30 — the paint/palette context ptr passed as the node's aux arg in several arms.

fields.name: rs_first
fields.reason: primary render-state +0x00 — the *pcVar1=='\0' early-out gate (0x1872d returns if 0). low byte = the entry's enabled flag/first field.

fields.name: rs_x
fields.reason: primary render-state +0x04 — the actor world X (dest base *(pcVar1+4); +0x40 anim-x is added at draw).

fields.name: rs_y
fields.reason: primary render-state +0x08 — the actor world Y (dest base *(pcVar1+8)).

fields.name: rs_kind2c
fields.reason: primary render-state +0x2c — the orientation/mode field the 0x1872d arm branches on (==3 = the &DAT_008a8440-indexed facing).

fields.name: rs_clip
fields.reason: primary render-state +0x6c — the animation CLIP ptr (anim_state.clip; non-null = animated, the 0x154-B 32-frame descriptor anim_clip pins). 0 = static.

fields.name: rs_frame
fields.reason: primary render-state +0x72 — current frame index (low u16; the renderer reads base+frame_delta[frame] for the sprite id). anim_state.frame.

fields.name: row0_bf
fields.reason: actor+0x48 — dir-0 sprite row word: low u16 = bank (0=>this dir draws nothing), high s16 = frame_base. FUN_0044d160 reads bank@+0x48, frame_base@+0x4a. THE code->sprite datum (the only spawn input not in map_data).

fields.name: row0_mx
fields.reason: actor+0x50 — dir-0 row mirror_x reference (used when render-state facing==3).

fields.name: row0_xoff
fields.reason: actor+0x54 — dir-0 row x placement offset (added to desc off_x when not mirrored/abs).

fields.name: row0_yoff
fields.reason: actor+0x58 — dir-0 row y placement offset (always added to desc off_y).

fields.name: dir_e8
fields.reason: actor+0xe8 — facing/dir (low u16; selects the +0x48 row). The spawn sets 0; confirm the static props stay 0 so dir-0 row is the rendered one.

fields.name: d1_bf
fields.reason: actor+0x5c — dir-1 row bank|frame_base word (table shape).

fields.name: d2_bf
fields.reason: actor+0x70 — dir-2 row bank|frame_base.

fields.name: d3_bf
fields.reason: actor+0x84 — dir-3 row bank|frame_base.

fields.name: d4_bf
fields.reason: actor+0x98 — dir-4 row bank|frame_base.

fields.name: d5_bf
fields.reason: actor+0xac — dir-5 row bank|frame_base.

fields.name: d6_bf
fields.reason: actor+0xc0 — dir-6 row bank|frame_base.

fields.name: d7_bf
fields.reason: actor+0xd4 — dir-7 row bank|frame_base.

fields.name: alpha_f4
fields.reason: actor+0xf4 — node alpha override (0 => desc alpha 0 => mode-0 keyed). The emit's param8.

fields.name: skip_284
fields.reason: actor+0x284 — non-zero => 0x491ae0 renders nothing (the skip flag).

fields.name: angle_ec
fields.reason: actor+0xec — non-zero => angle-driven frame select (town props expected 0).

fields.name: rs_dstx
fields.reason: primary render-state +0x40 — dst base x added to desc off_x at emit.

fields.name: rs_dsty
fields.reason: primary render-state +0x44 — dst base y added to desc off_y at emit.

fields.name: rs_lo284
fields.reason: primary render-state +0x284 — layer override sub-object ptr (non-null => layer = *(it+0x100)); expect 0 for town props.

0x560e60.name: actor_slot_reset
0x560e60._note: FUN_00560e60 (42 B) — per-actor SLOT RESET (zeros actor dwords [0],[1],[2],[0x271],[0x27c..0x27e] + u16 [0x272]); __thiscall ECX = the actor being (re)spawned. Called at EVERY actor spawn — so its `ret_va` (auto, an RVA; +0x400000 = absolute) names the SPAWN caller. SPAWN-DISCOVERY probe (ckpt 76): drive to game_enter, whitelist flip 1434 (the load runs inside 0x59f2c0 with no Flip yet), read the distinct ret_va to find who populates the room actor band DAT_008a9b50+0x11e0. Distinguishes the 8 party actors (ret_va in 0x59f2c0) from the room/NPC band spawn (ret_va elsewhere).

fields.name: who
fields.reason: actor+0x00 — presence/identity of the actor being reset (forces the VA to hook; the real datum is the auto ret_va = the spawn call site).

0x584710.name: actor_manifest_build
0x584710._note: FUN_00584710 (2280 B) — a suspected room ACTOR-MANIFEST builder (calls 0x560e60; builds ~12 ~0x50-byte stack descriptors). SPAWN-DISCOVERY probe (ckpt 76): does it fire at game_enter, and what is its ret_va (caller)? Confirms/refutes it as the town-NPC spawner + maps the spawn call chain (god-object DAT_008a9b50+0x11e0 population).

fields.name: who
fields.reason: ECX+0x00 — presence (forces the hook; the datum is the auto ret_va).

0x5b9a40.name: blt_onto
0x5b9a40._note: FUN_005b9a40 — generic Blt self-onto-dest (__thiscall: ECX = SOURCE cel, args[0] = dest, args[1]/[2] = dest x/y). The width/height come from the source's metrics (+0xb8/+0xbc), not args. Port mirror: src/zdd.c zdd_object_blt_onto + zdd_emit_blit(0x5b9a40). DDraw blit trace (Phase-B B3) — diffed by tools/render_diff.py keyed on (res, frame). Used by the parallax far-plane sink (game_parallax_blit).

0x5b9b70.name: blt_keyed
0x5b9b70._note: FUN_005b9b70 — positioned color-keyed Blt (__thiscall: ECX = SOURCE cel, args[0] = dest, args[1]/[2] = dest x/y). dst origin = metric_0c/_10 + (x,y); w/h from metric_b8/_bc. Port mirror: zdd_object_blt_keyed + zdd_emit_blit(0x5b9b70). Used by the prologue keyed-fallback path.

0x5b9ae0.name: blt_rects
0x5b9ae0._note: FUN_005b9ae0 — Blt with explicit rects (__thiscall: ECX = SOURCE cel, args[0] = dest, args[1..6] = dst_x, dst_y, dst_w, dst_h, src_x, src_y). Port mirror: zdd_object_blt_rects + zdd_emit_blit(0x5b9ae0). Used by the alpha orchestrator's scratch->dest leg.

0x5b9bf0.name: blt_clipped
0x5b9bf0._note: FUN_005b9bf0 — clipped color-keyed Blt, THE backdrop-tile path (__thiscall: ECX = SOURCE cel, args[0] = dest, args[1..6] = dst_x, dst_y, width, height, src_x, src_y). The source rect is clipped against the cel's region metrics (+0x0c/+0x10 origin, +0x14/+0x18 extent); we emit the RAW inputs (clip math is a bit-exact port). Port mirror: zdd_object_blt_clipped + zdd_emit_blit(0x5b9bf0). map_present mode-3 -> game_present_blit -> this.

0x4937c0.name: band_1060_render
0x4937c0._note: FUN_004937c0 (647 B) — render/emit pass for the actor band DAT_008a9b50+0x1060 (0x40 slots), called per active slot (actor+0x1d0!=0) from the per-frame driver FUN_0048c150:60. __thiscall ECX=the per-slot actor (same layout as the main band: +0x1d4 code, +0x40->render-state). CENSUS hook to find which band emits the establishing-hold cast (banks 0x426/0x459/0x462/0x46a/0x46b/0x472/0x47b chars, 0x481 tree, 0x3fa details) — phase-1 of the in-game-intro 1:1 arc.

0x493ba0.name: band_1160_char_render
0x493ba0._note: FUN_00493ba0 (3014 B) — the multi-part CHARACTER renderer: render/emit for the band DAT_008a9b50+0x1160 (0x20 slots; ckpt-78 EFFECT 50k range) from FUN_0048c150:92, AND the per-party-member renderer called from FUN_004997b0 (the 8-slot party loop, FUN_0048c150:101-102). Distinguish the two callers by ret_va. ECX=per-slot character object (+0x1d4 code = in_ECX[0x75], +0x40->render-state = in_ECX[0x10]; param_1=sub-slot, 0 at both call sites). Core render = the already-ported FUN_0044d160 descriptor + FUN_00492670 emit (+ shadow/color-split via FUN_004917b0). CENSUS hook (see band_1060_render).

Set by the dispatcher FUN_0058d460:96 cVar12 = (puVar1[4]!=0)?3:1 from the MAP sub-record puVar1[4] (NOT RNG), passed as param_8 to FUN_0041f200/FUN_00431e30. Phase-2 census: read per townsperson to port the per-code facing (the USER 'flipped orientation' residual).

0x492670.name: draw_pool_emit_actor
0x492670._note: FUN_00492670 (118 B) — the actor draw-pool node emitter (mode bool(param_7!=0)). __thiscall ECX=view; args[0]=layer, args[1]=cel ptr, args[2]=world x, args[3]=world y, args[4]=off x, args[5]=off y, args[6]=alpha. cel_res = the emitted cel's resource_id (renderid index 1). Hooked to pin which band emits each establishing-hold cel (e.g. res 0x481 the tree). Port mirror = draw_pool_emit_actor.

0x4917b0.name: draw_pool_emit_layered
0x4917b0._note: FUN_004917b0 (106 B) — the alternate draw-pool node emitter used by the multi-part char renderer (0x493ba0) + the device band (0x493480) for shadow/mirror/color-split layers. __thiscall ECX=view; args[0]=layer, args[1]=mode (node+0x18), args[2]=cel ptr, args[3]=x, args[4]=y, args[5]=off x, args[6]=off y, args[7]=alpha(node+0x14). cel_res = renderid index 2.

fields.reason: alpha (param_8 -> node+0x14); the fountain particle 0x18708 blend level (FUN_00493480 passes actor+0xf4). 0 => clipped, !=0 => alpha-blend.

0x499ab0.name: scene_cinematic_step
0x499ab0._note: FUN_00499ab0 (1356 B) — the in-game SCENE CINEMATIC controller step, called once/sim-tick from 0x439690:1124 (right after the camera easer 0x43d1d0:1123) and from the cutscene drivers 0x55f190:136 / 0x58f360:478. Its __thiscall this holds the LETTERBOX bar heights (+0x44/+0x48) + scroll (+0x4c), ramped ±2/call toward targets (mode +0x40: 0 => toward 0; !=0 => +0x44/+0x48 toward 0x40=64, +0x4c toward +0x50). LIVE-MEASURED (ckpt 90): during the opening-town establishing shot these bars are CONSTANT 64 (mode 1, already settled) and scroll is 0 — so the letterbox is fixed 64px (= the constant grid-fill 0x48c48a/0x48c4fe) and 0x499ab0 is NOT the establishing REVEAL. The REVEAL is a SEPARATE per-cell FADE-GRID at *(0x8a9b50+0x1040). CORRECTED ckpt 95 (the ckpt-90 attribution of the update to FUN_0049af40 was WRONG — reading 0x49af40 it is the HUD/portrait/HP-bar animator, walks the party array room+0x4030, returns a counter): the grid UPDATE is the INLINE loop here at 0x499ab0:125-177 (advance each fading cell's timer; the iris PATTERN is set by FUN_0049a890 (variant 0)/0x49a740 (1)/0x49aae0+0x49aa00 (2)), and the grid is RENDERED by FUN_0048e920 (via 0x48c150:175). 1x/sim-tick (mode 1 advances 2 rows/tick = the measured -8px/sim-tick). PORTED ckpt 95: src/scene_fade.c. Fields read at onEnter off ECX. R6-B GRID DUMP (ckpt 106): the sf_/ovl_/r40..r80 chain fields read the fade-GRID object (*(0x8a9b50+0x1040)) at onEnter = state BEFORE this tick's overlay-level update + iris setter/aging. r<N> = column-0 cell of grid row N packed u32 (state | timer<<16); cells base *(grid+0), stride 0xc, W=10. Port mirror: main.c CALL_TRACE_BEGIN(0x499ab0) right before scene_fade_step.

fields.name: mode
fields.reason: this+0x40 — the ramp MODE: 0 => bars retract toward 0 (no letterbox); !=0 => bars ramp toward 0x40=64 (the cinematic letterbox).

fields.name: bot_h
fields.reason: this+0x44 — BOTTOM letterbox bar height (read by 0x48c150 as in_ECX[0x11]); bottom bar = rows [480-h..480]. Ramps ±2/call toward 64.

fields.name: top_h
fields.reason: this+0x48 — TOP letterbox bar height (read by 0x48c150 as in_ECX[0x12]); top bar = rows [0..h]. Ramps ±2/call toward 64. The establishing-shot REVEAL = this ramping ~240->64.

fields.name: scroll
fields.reason: this+0x4c — the vertical scroll accumulator written to view+0x74 (used by the tilemap renderer 0x490f30 row origin). Ramps ±2/call toward this+0x50.

fields.name: sf_rad
fields._note: low u16 = grid radius (+0x14); high u16 = +0x16 (mask in analysis)

fields.name: ovl_mode
fields._note: grid[8] — the OVERLAY screen-fade mode (1=zero level, 2=ramp level to 1000, 3=ramp to 0), updated at 0x499ab0:104-124 BEFORE the iris setter/aging; armed by the beat-runner +0x30 request (0x439690:585+). NOT modelled by the port (R6-B suspect).

fields.name: ovl_speed
fields._note: grid[9] — overlay fade speed; per-tick rate = speed*20/1000

fields.name: ovl_level
fields._note: grid[10] — the overlay screen-fade LEVEL 0..1000, consumed by ~12 renderers as (level-1000)*3000/1000 + X (whole-scene darkening: 0x407ba0/0x40a010/0x40ac90/0x40bb90/0x46d180/0x46d4a0/0x46e510:1771/0x489280/0x554570/0x557060/0x54d090).

0x48e920.name: scene_fade_grid_render
0x48e920._note: FUN_0048e920 (403 B) — the SCENE-TRANSITION FADE-GRID render (the establishing REVEAL producer, ckpt 90). Walks a grid of per-cell records (this[0] = cell array, this[2] = cell count, this[3] = mode 1/2/3) stride 0xc: cell+0=state(0/1/2), cell+2=fade timer, cell+4=col (<<6 => dest x), cell+8=row (<<2 => dest y, 4px rows). Per cell, by mode+state it either ALPHA-blits the 64x4 black cel (res 0x583) via FUN_005bd550 (alpha = 0x1f-(timer<<5)/1000, the partial-fade case, ret 0x48e9c3-region) or OPAQUE-blits it via FUN_005b9a40 (ret 0x48e9c3, the fully-black case) or skips (clear). The cel-by-alpha-level table is *(0x8a76e0) (slot 40 = res 0x458, frame=alpha); the opaque cel is *(0x8a76e4) (slot 41 = res 0x583). Called from the in-game render driver 0x48c150:175 (FUN_0048e920(*(0x8a93cc+0x16c) = the paint_ctx dest); this/ECX = the grid *(0x8a9b50+0x1040)), AFTER the constant-64 letterbox bars. LIVE (ckpt 95, runs/reveal-grid): grid W=10 H=120 count=1200, mode=1, speed=1000, variant=0 (center-out). At game_enter ~1490 black tiles shrinking to 320 (the letterbox only) by ~sim_tick 25 = the center-out vertical iris. PORTED ckpt 95: src/scene_fade.c (render scene_fade_render; update = the 0x499ab0 inline loop + 0x49a8xx setters; arm = 0x439690:555-583). ret_va 0x48e9c3 on FUN_005b9a40 is THE signature of this producer in a 0x5b9a40 blit trace.

fields.name: ncells
fields.reason: this[2] — number of grid cells walked (the screen-covering 64x4 tile grid; ~1200 full).

fields.name: mode
fields.reason: this[3] — fade mode (1/2/3): selects alpha-fade-in vs fade-out vs opaque per-cell branch.

fields.name: variant
fields.reason: this[4] — iris VARIANT, RNG-chosen at arm (0x439690:563, (rand*3)>>15 in {0,1,2}): 0=center-out vertical (0x49a890), 1=edges-in vertical (0x49a740), 2=single vertical sweep (0x49aae0/0x49aa00).

fields.name: speed
fields.reason: this[7] — fade speed (request+0x2c at arm 0x439690:564); pattern/timer step = speed*100/1000 = speed/10 per sim-tick. The per-cell alpha fade rate (NOT the row-advance, which is mode's 2/4 rows/tick).

fields.name: wh
fields.reason: low16 = W (cols, dest x = col<<6), high16 = H (rows, dest y = row<<2). count this[2] = W*H. Expect W=10 (640/64), H=120 (480/4) => 1200.

fields.name: reveal_desc
fields.reason: RESOLVED (ckpt 95): the alpha blend descriptor the reveal's 0x5bd550 uses (ECX from 0x48ea67 = [0x8a93b8]) = the [19]/full entry of the group-E ramp table 0x8a936c. UNIQUE vs ramp_a[19](0x8a9304)/ramp_b[19](0x8a9354) — runs/reveal-desc. The port builds it: g_pd_boot_group_e[19] (weight 1000, mode-2 subtract-blend). Wired into game_scene_fade_alpha.

fields.name: rampa19
fields.reason: ramp_a[19] (the port's g_ramp_a full entry, 0x8a92b8 table). Alias check vs reveal_desc.

fields.name: rampb19
fields.reason: ramp_b[19] (the port's g_ramp_b full entry, 0x8a9308 table). Alias check vs reveal_desc.

0x49af40.name: hud_party_anim_update
0x49af40._note: FUN_0049af40 (3313 B) — the per-frame HUD/PORTRAIT/HP-BAR animator (NOT the fade grid — ckpt-90 mislabeled it 'scene_fade_grid_update'; CORRECTED ckpt 95). Called 2x/sim-tick from 0x499ab0:180-183. Walks the 8 party slots (room+0x4030, local_8=0x4030 stepped +4 x8 at :505-558), lerps HP/MP-ratio fill bars ((+0x54*1000)/(+0x84+0x9c+0x58)) + portrait/icon fade timers (+0xe2/+0xe4/+0xf6 etc.) toward targets via FUN_0049bdf0, and returns a rolling counter ((uVar13+3)/100). The establishing REVEAL fade-grid update is instead the inline loop at 0x499ab0:125-177 + the FUN_0049a8xx pattern setters (ported src/scene_fade.c). Left annotated for the eventual HUD port.

0x5531b0.name: ambient_sound_timer
0x5531b0._note: FUN_005531b0 (756 B) — the ambient-event SOUND timer (a per-object random-event countdown). Called from FUN_0054f980 cases 0x1136f (x2)/0x11370 (the town's ambient sound emitters, map CHARACTER codes 70511/70512). param_1 (arg0) = the render-state carrying the countdown +0x5c (int) + first-call init-flag +0x58 (short); param_2 (arg1) = the gate (the body runs only when param_2==0 AND scene-lock global+0x27a8==0 AND sound globals +0x2770==0/+0x2764!=1). First call (+0x58==0): +0x5c=(rand*param_4>>15) [DRAW]. Then if +0x5c<1: reload +0x5c=(rand*param_4>>15)+param_3 [DRAW], pick (rand*param_9>>15) a wave [DRAW], conditional sound-param [DRAW] — else +0x5c--. Captured to pin the per-tick decrement cadence: the 0x5531b0 fires (ticks 33/189, +3 each) do NOT fit unit cadence from the tick-0 init countdowns (C=141 for 0x1136f / C=189 for 0x11370) — the decrement is gated. engine-quirk #95; fountain-rng-phase.

fields.name: obj
fields.reason: param_1 = the render-state ptr (distinguishes the two timer objects).

fields.name: gate
fields.reason: param_2 = the gate; the timer body runs only when this is 0.

fields.name: cd
fields.reason: *(param_1+0x5c) at onEnter = the countdown BEFORE this call's decrement/fire. Decreases 1/active-tick; fires (reload) at <1.

fields.name: initf
fields.reason: *(param_1+0x58) low u16 = the first-call init flag (0 -> inits +0x5c this call, then 1 forever).

0x467380.name: ambient_event_timer
0x467380._note: FUN_00467380 (376 B, __thiscall ECX=obj) — a periodic event timer. +0x208 = enable, +0x20c = countdown (short). When +0x20c==1: fire (DRAW 500-(rand*1000>>15)) + conditional FUN_004099a0 (which draws). When +0x20c==0: reload +0x20c=(rand*1000)%param_4 + param_3 [DRAW]. Else +0x20c--. Captured to pin the tick-183 fire (+4 draws: fire + 2x 0x4099a0 + reload). engine-quirk #95; fountain-rng-phase.

fields.name: enable
fields.reason: in_ECX+0x208 — the timer-enable flag (body runs only when !=0).

fields.name: cd
fields.reason: in_ECX+0x20c low u16 = the countdown; fires at 1, reloads at 0.

0x48cf80.name: widget_box_frame
0x48cf80._note: FUN_0048cf80 — the widget 9-slice box-frame tiler (newgame panels + the in-game dialogue bubble). Args 1-4 = the draw rect; during the dialogue pop-in (0x48c820 node mode +0x1c==1) w/h are the +0x54-scaled extent, so a cross-side diff here names a pop-in phase/geometry divergence directly. Port mirror: src/main.c game_render_dialogue (CALL_TRACE_BEGIN(0x48cf80)).
